summary: Matched more of the Dolphin VI implementation by correcting VI globals/layout, timing tables, current timing/state tracking, DTV status typing/implementation, and several VI register/state paths. The PR also updated a game caller to treat VIGetDTVStatus as an integer status and added the PAD/SIRefreshSamplingRate prototype needed by VI retrace handling.
what_changed: Reordered and resized VI .sbss symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt: shdwChangeMode moved to 0x804D7440, changed became the 8-byte object at 0x804D7448, changeMode moved to 0x804D7450, and shdwChanged became the 8-byte object at 0x804D7458.
In vi.c, introduced static volatile u32 shdwChangeMode and moved static volatile u32 changeMode to match the corrected symbol layout around the 64-bit changed and shdwChanged masks.
Expanded the static VITiming table from 7 to 8 entries, adjusted the NTSC progressive timing row, and added an additional progressive-style timing row used by mode 3.

Added CurrTiming and CurrTvMode globals and used them in VISetRegs, VIInit, getCurrentHalfLine, getCurrentFieldEvenOdd, VIGetCurrentLine, and VIGetTvFormat instead of repeatedly deriving timing from HorVer or hardware registers.
Added getTiming cases for raw mode values 20, 21, 3, 16, and 17, alongside the existing VI_TVMODE_* enum cases.
Updated __VIInit so mode 3 follows the progressive-register path like VI_TVMODE_NTSC_PROG.

Changed VIInit ordering so dspCfg/HorVer timing is imported and CurrTiming/CurrTvMode are initialized before setting display size/position; DispSizeY now comes from CurrTiming->acv << 1 & 0xFFFE and DispPosY is initialized to 0.
Changed setVerticalRegs black-screen vertical math to add actualAcv * 2 - 2 rather than dispSizeY - 2.
Changed VIFlush to merge shdwChangeMode into changeMode and clear shdwChangeMode before copying changed bits into shdwChanged.
Added SIRefreshSamplingRate() call after VISetRegs succeeds in __VIRetraceHandler, requiring inclusion of dolphin/pad.h and a new prototype in extern/dolphin/include/dolphin/pad.h.

Changed VIGetTvFormat to disable interrupts, map CurrTvMode to returned format values, and restore interrupts before return; the diff still shows the ASSERTMSGLINE using format before assignment, so the excerpt alone does not prove whether surrounding code/macros make that safe.
Implemented u32 VIGetDTVStatus(void) in vi.c by reading __VIRegs[55] & 3 under interrupt disable/restore and returning bit 0.
Changed the public declaration of VIGetDTVStatus from void* to u32 in vifuncs.h.
Updated gm_801A4510 in src/melee/gm/gm_1A3F.c to compare VIGetDTVStatus() against 0 instead of NULL.
smart_moves: Fixing the symbol map and C static declaration order together addressed the likely root of .sbss layout mismatch instead of only forcing local code shape.
Using explicit 64-bit volatile masks for changed and shdwChanged preserved the MARK_CHANGED bitmask semantics and aligned the objects with their actual 8-byte symbol sizes.
Adding CurrTiming/CurrTvMode cached state simplified multiple query functions and avoided recomputing active timing from register reads in paths that need to match SDK behavior.
Changing VIGetDTVStatus to u32 and fixing the caller from NULL to 0 made the API reflect status-bit semantics rather than pointer semantics.
Calling SIRefreshSamplingRate only after VISetRegs succeeds placed controller sampling-rate refresh at the point where VI register changes are actually applied.
Adding raw numeric mode cases to getTiming allowed matching observed SDK/game mode values without inventing new enum names in the diff.
decomp_lessons: For Dolphin SDK modules, .sbss symbol order and object size can reveal incorrect C declarations; a misplaced u32 before volatile u64 globals can cascade into wrong labels and artificial padding.
When a status API returns a hardware bit, prefer integer return types and integer comparisons; pointer-like prototypes can hide mismatches and produce misleading game-side code.

Cached current hardware mode/timing globals can be necessary for matching SDK VI behavior, especially when query functions must reflect the last applied VI mode rather than reconstructing state from several registers.
Mode aliases may appear as raw numeric cases in retail SDK code; preserving those numeric cases can be a better matching tactic than prematurely naming unsupported enum values.
Interrupt disable/restore patterns around VI state reads are part of the matching surface and should be preserved even for small getter functions.
Header fixes and caller fixes should travel with implementation matches when a prototype correction changes source-level comparisons or include requirements.
naming_conventions: Existing SDK-style globals use mixed case names such as CurrTiming, CurrTvMode, FBSet, HorVer, PreCB, and PostCB.
Shadow-state VI globals use the shdw prefix, as in shdwChangeMode, shdwChanged, and shdwRegs.
Bit-mask macro naming uses all caps with an action phrase: MARK_CHANGED(index).
Public Dolphin functions retain SDK-style prefixes such as VIGetDTVStatus, VIGetTvFormat, VIFlush, VIInit, and SIRefreshSamplingRate.
The diff keeps unidentified timing mode aliases as numeric switch cases rather than introducing speculative names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched .sbss by reordering static declarations and correcting 32-bit versus 64-bit object sizes for changeMode/shdwChangeMode and changed/shdwChanged.
Used volatile qualifiers on VI state touched by retrace/flush paths to preserve expected memory-access behavior.
Introduced CurrTiming and CurrTvMode to reduce register-derived recomputation and match getter code shape.
Used cntlzd-driven changed-bit copying in VIFlush while adding shdwChangeMode merge before shdwChanged propagation.
Added exact hardware-register bit extraction in VIGetDTVStatus: read __VIRegs[55] & 3, restore interrupts, return low bit.
Adjusted timing constants and table cardinality rather than compensating at call sites.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided slice.
The context reports one issue comment, but its body was not present in the excerpt, so no reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching VIGetTvFormat to see whether the ASSERTMSGLINE placement around format was further adjusted.
Search for additional Dolphin VI matching PRs that rename or formalize raw getTiming mode cases 3, 16, 17, 20, and 21.
Check whether symbols around 0x804D7440-0x804D7458 remained stable after this PR.
Search for SIRefreshSamplingRate callers to confirm whether VI retrace is the only SDK-matching call site added in this period.
